title: Privacy Minimum Data Loop
summary: Reduces a workflow to the minimum personal or sensitive data needed for the stated purpose.
steps: State the purpose, users, outputs, retention needs, and downstream consumers for the workflow.
Classify each data field by sensitivity and necessity for the purpose.
Remove, hash, aggregate, redact, or delay collection for fields that are not strictly needed.
Test that the workflow still meets its purpose with reduced data.
Update documentation, retention settings, and access controls to match the minimized design.
stopConditions: business owner accepts residual data need
author: Palette Lab
prompt:
Use the Privacy Minimum Data Loop for [workflow, data_fields, purpose].

First define the task boundary, success criteria, allowed actions, required evidence, risk level, and budget. Work in bounded iterations. In each iteration:
- State the purpose, users, outputs, retention needs, and downstream consumers for the workflow.
- Classify each data field by sensitivity and necessity for the purpose.
- Remove, hash, aggregate, redact, or delay collection for fields that are not strictly needed.
- Test that the workflow still meets its purpose with reduced data.
- Update documentation, retention settings, and access controls to match the minimized design.

Quality gates before completion:
- purpose stated
- field necessity justified
- sensitive data minimized
- workflow still works
- retention and access updated

Stop when one of these is true:
- minimum viable data set proven
- legal review needed
- business owner accepts residual data need

Finish with:
- field inventory
- before-after data flow
- test output
- retention and access notes

Escalate for human approval before: processing special-category data, changing retention policy, sharing data externally.
